samples.gvd:
module dual_port_ram #(parameter DATA_WIDTH = 8, parameter ADDR_WIDTH = 4)(
    input wclk,
    input rclk,
    input wenc,
    input [ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] waddr,
    input [DATA_WIDTH-1:0] wdata,
    input rinc,
    input [ADDR_WIDTH-1:0] raddr,
    output reg [DATA_WIDTH-1:0] rdata
);

reg [DATA_WIDTH-1:0] ram [0:(1<<ADDR_WIDTH)-1];

always @(posedge wclk) begin
    if (wenc) begin
        ram[waddr] <= wdata;
    end
end

always @(posedge rclk) begin
    if (rinc) begin
        rdata <= ram[raddr];
    end
end

endmodule
samples.xml_description:
Implement an asynchronous FIFO, FIFO bit width and depth can be configured(parameter DEPTH = 16, parameter WIDTH = 8). The asynchronous FIFO structure is divided into several parts. The first part is dual-port RAM, which is used for data storage. Instantiate dual-port RAM as a submodule, The RAM ports are input wclk, input wenc, input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] waddr, input [WIDTH-1:0] wdata, input rclk, input renc, input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] raddr, output reg [WIDTH-1:0] rdata. The second part is the data write controller. The third part is the data read controller. The fourth part is the read pointer synchronizer. The read pointer is collected using the two-stage trigger of the write clock and output to the data write controller. The fifth part is the write pointer synchronizer, which uses the two-stage trigger of the read clock to collect the write pointer and output it to the data read controller. Write clock. Read clock. Reset signal. Write increment. Read increment. Write data. Write full. Read empty. Read data. Data width of FIFO. Depth of FIFO.

Asynchronous FIFO is composed of four parts. First is dual-port RAM, which is used for data storage. Instantiate dual-port RAM as a submodule, The RAM ports are input wclk, input wenc, input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] waddr, input [WIDTH-1:0] wdata, input rclk, input renc, input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0] raddr, output reg [WIDTH-1:0] rdata.

The second part is the data write controller. The third part is the data read controller. The fourth part is the read pointer synchronizer. The read pointer is collected using the two-stage trigger of the write clock and output to the data write controller. The fifth part is the write pointer synchronizer, which uses the two-stage trigger of the read clock to collect the write pointer and output it to the data read controller.

Give the dual-port RAM module a name of dual_port_RAM, with input wclk, wenc, waddr, wdata, rclk, renc, raddr, output reg rdata. The dual-port RAM module is instantiated in the asyn_fifo module.

The data write controller is responsible for controlling the write operation. It takes winc, waddr, rptr_syn as inputs and outputs wptr, wfull. The write controller uses the write increment signal to increment the write pointer. If the FIFO is not full, the write controller writes the data to the RAM module. If the FIFO is full, the write controller does not write the data to the RAM module.

The data read controller is responsible for controlling the read operation. It takes rinc, wptr, raddr as inputs and outputs rptr, rempty. The read controller uses the read increment signal to increment the read pointer. If the FIFO is not empty, the read controller reads the data from the RAM module. If the FIFO is empty, the read controller does not read the data from the RAM module.

The read pointer synchronizer is responsible for synchronizing the read pointer to the write clock. It takes rptr, wclk as inputs and outputs rptr_syn. The read pointer synchronizer uses the two-stage trigger of the write clock to collect the read pointer.

The write pointer synchronizer is responsible for synchronizing the write pointer to the read clock. It takes wptr, rclk as inputs and outputs wptr_syn. The write pointer synchronizer uses the two-stage trigger of the read clock to collect the write pointer.

Give the data write controller a name of write_control, with input winc, waddr, rptr_syn, output wptr, wfull. The write controller is implemented using a 4-bit binary counter. The counter is reset when rst_n is 0. The counter is incremented when winc is 1. The counter is not incremented when winc is 0. The counter is reset to 0 when rst_n is 0.

Give the data read controller a name of read_control, with input rinc, wptr, raddr, output rptr, rempty. The read controller is implemented using a 4-bit binary counter. The counter is reset when rst_n is 0. The counter is incremented when rinc is 1. The counter is not incremented when rinc is 0. The counter is reset to 0 when rst_n is 0.

Give the read pointer synchronizer a name of rptr_syn, with input rptr, wclk, output rptr_syn. The read pointer synchronizer is implemented using a 4-bit binary register. The register is reset when rst_n is 0. The register is updated with rptr when wclk is 1. The register is not updated with rptr when wclk is 0. The register is reset to 0 when rst_n is 0.

Give the write pointer synchronizer a name of wptr_syn, with input wptr, rclk, output wptr_syn. The write pointer synchronizer is implemented using a 4-bit binary register. The register is reset when rst_n is 0. The register is updated with wptr when rclk is 1. The register is not updated with wptr when rclk is 0. The register is reset to 0 when rst_n is 0.

The last part is the empty and full signal. The full signal is wfull, it is 1 when the FIFO is full. The empty signal is rempty, it is 1 when the FIFO is empty. The full signal is generated by comparing the write pointer and the read pointer. The read pointer is rptr_syn, the write pointer is wptr. The full signal is generated by comparing the write pointer and the read pointer. If the write pointer is equal to the read pointer, the FIFO is empty. If the write pointer is not equal to the read pointer, the FIFO is not empty.

Give the empty and full signal a name of full_empty, with input wptr, rptr, output wfull, rempty. The full signal is 1 when the FIFO is full. The empty signal is 1 when the FIFO is empty. The full signal is generated by comparing the write pointer and the read pointer. The full signal is generated by comparing the write pointer and the read pointer.

Give the input and output signal a name of input_output, with input winc, rinc, wdata, output wfull, rempty, rdata. The output signal is rdata, it is the data read from the FIFO. The output signal is rempty, it is 1 when the FIFO is empty. The output signal is wfull, it is 1 when the FIFO is full.
